questions.q: Who was Nakula's mother?
questions.explanation: Madri, Pandu's second wife and princess of Madra, bore the twins Nakula and Sahadeva.

questions.q: Which twin gods fathered Nakula and his brother?
questions.explanation: Madri summoned the Ashvins, the twin physician gods of the dawn, and bore Nakula and Sahadeva.

questions.q: Who was Nakula's twin brother?
questions.explanation: Nakula was the elder of the twins, born moments before his brother Sahadeva.

questions.q: Nakula was famed throughout the epic as what?
options: The strongest man of his age
The most beautiful man of his age
questions.explanation: Nakula was celebrated as the most handsome man of his time, a renown that would later prove his one flaw.

questions.q: Which two skills was Nakula especially celebrated for?
questions.explanation: Nakula was a master of horses and one of the finest swordsmen among the Pandavas.

questions.q: What name did Nakula use while serving King Virata during the year in hiding?
questions.explanation: Nakula became Granthika, the keeper of King Virata's horses, a disguise that matched his true gifts.

questions.q: What was Nakula's position by birth among the five Pandavas?
questions.explanation: Nakula was the fourth Pandava, younger than Kunti's three sons and older than his twin Sahadeva.

questions.q: Which queen was the shared wife of Nakula and his four brothers?
questions.explanation: Draupadi, won at her swayamvara, became the common wife of all five Pandavas including Nakula.

questions.q: Who raised Nakula and his twin after their mother died on Pandu's pyre?
questions.explanation: Madri entrusted the twins to Kunti, who raised them without distinction from her own three sons.

questions.q: Which king of Hastinapura was Nakula's earthly father?
questions.explanation: Nakula was a son of King Pandu, born through the Ashvins because of the curse that barred Pandu from fathering children.

questions.q: When the Yaksha let Yudhishthira revive a single fallen brother, why did he choose Nakula?
options: Nakula was the youngest and most defenseless
Nakula had fallen first and waited longest
Nakula alone knew the way out of the forest
So that Madri, like Kunti, would still have one living son
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira chose fairness over advantage, asking that each of his father's wives keep a living son, and the Yaksha revived all four brothers.

questions.q: Which direction did Nakula conquer for Yudhishthira's Rajasuya sacrifice?
questions.explanation: Nakula led the western campaign, returning with such tribute that ten thousand camels could barely carry it.

questions.q: Who was Nakula's son by Draupadi, one of the five Upapandavas?
questions.explanation: Shatanika was Nakula's son by Draupadi, named after a famed royal sage of the Kuru line.

questions.q: Which princess of Chedi became Nakula's other wife?
questions.explanation: Nakula married Karenumati of the Chedi royal house, who bore him a son.

questions.q: Which king of Madra was Nakula's maternal uncle?
questions.explanation: Shalya, brother of Madri, was uncle to the twins yet was tricked into fighting on the Kaurava side.

questions.q: Why did Karna spare Nakula after defeating him in the war?
options: He had promised Kunti to kill no Pandava but Arjuna
He pitied the youth of Madri's son
Duryodhana had ordered the twins taken alive
He wished to shame the Pandavas with mercy
questions.explanation: Bound by his word to Kunti that her five sons would survive him, Karna disarmed Nakula and let him go with only mocking words.

questions.q: What was the name of Nakula's conch?
questions.explanation: Nakula sounded the conch Sughosha at Kurukshetra, while his twin Sahadeva blew the Manipushpaka.

questions.q: According to Yudhishthira, what flaw caused Nakula to fall on the final journey north?
options: Pride in his own matchless beauty
Attachment to his horses and wealth
Doubt in the path to heaven
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira explained that Nakula fell because he believed no one in the world equalled him in beauty.

questions.q: Where was Nakula born during Pandu's forest retreat?
questions.explanation: All five Pandavas, including Nakula, were born at Shatashringa, where Pandu lived after renouncing the throne.

questions.q: Which sage's mantra, shared by Kunti, allowed Madri to summon the gods who fathered Nakula?
questions.explanation: The mantra came from the sage Durvasa, who had granted it to Kunti for her hospitality, and Kunti lent it to Madri at Pandu's request.

questions.q: In the dice game at Hastinapura, which brother did Yudhishthira stake first?
questions.explanation: When his wealth and kingdom were gone, Yudhishthira staked Nakula first among his brothers, and Shakuni's dice took him.

questions.q: At the poisoned lake of the Yaksha, which Pandava was sent first for water and fell?
questions.explanation: Nakula went first to fetch water, ignored the invisible guardian's warning, drank, and fell dead by the shore.

questions.q: On the war's final day, Nakula slew Chitrasena, Satyasena and Sushena, the sons of which warrior?
questions.explanation: In the Shalya Parva, Nakula cut down three of Karna's sons on the eighteenth day of battle.

questions.q: Who was Nakula's son by his Chedi wife Karenumati?
questions.explanation: Karenumati bore Nakula a son named Niramitra, meaning one without enemies.

questions.q: After the war, whose mansion in Hastinapura was granted to Nakula?
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira allotted the fallen Kauravas' palaces to his brothers, and Nakula received the mansion of Durmarshana.

questions.q: During his western campaign before the Rajasuya, which kinsman's kingdom did Nakula bring under Yudhishthira's sway?
options: Madra, realm of his uncle Shalya
questions.explanation: Nakula's march west took him to Madra, where his uncle Shalya accepted Yudhishthira's overlordship with affection.

questions.q: On the final journey to the Himalayas, who fell immediately before Nakula?
questions.explanation: The order of falling was Draupadi, then Sahadeva, then Nakula, followed later by Arjuna and Bhima.

questions.q: How did Nakula's son Shatanika die?
options: Slain by Ashwatthama in the night raid on the sleeping camp
Killed inside the Chakravyuha beside Abhimanyu
Struck down by Karna on the seventeenth day
Burned in the house of lac at Varanavata
questions.explanation: Shatanika died with the other four sons of Draupadi when Ashwatthama butchered the sleeping Pandava camp after the war's last day.

questions.q: How did Madri gain two sons though Kunti's mantra was shared with her for a single use?
options: She secretly repeated the mantra a second time
Pandu persuaded Kunti to grant a second summons
She invoked the twin Ashvins, who came as a pair
The gods blessed her with twins unasked
questions.explanation: Madri cleverly called upon the twin Ashvins with her one invocation, receiving two sons at once, which is why Kunti declined to share the mantra again.

questions.q: In the Svargarohana Parva, each Pandava merged into the god who had fathered him; into whom did Nakula and his twin merge?
questions.explanation: In the epic's final book the twins were seen united with the Ashvins, the divine sources from whom they had been born.
